claims.evidence_text: the average TKW increased from 30.16 g TK−1 in the 1920s to 38.43 g TK−1 in the 2010s
claims.claim_summary: Chinese wheat average TKW rose from 30.16 g (1920s) to 38.43 g (2010s).

claims.evidence_text: Grain filling has a heat duration of approximately 700 growing degree-days (GDD). Hence, grain filling is shortened if the ambient temperature is particularly high during this period
claims.claim_summary: Wheat grain filling requires approximately 700 GDD.

claims.evidence_text: At temperatures between 20°C and 30°C, the grain filling rate slightly increases with temperature ... At temperatures > 30°C, the grain filling rate slows down ... because of thermal damage
claims.claim_summary: Wheat grain filling rate increases with temperature up to 30C; above 30C it slows due to thermal damage.

claims.evidence_text: At temperatures > 30°C, the grain filling rate slows down in part because of thermal damage to the plant organs and accelerated plant senescence
claims.claim_summary: Temperatures above 30C cause thermal damage and accelerated senescence during wheat grain filling.

claims.evidence_text: The sowing density, row spacing, sowing depth, and plot size were 270 plants m -2 , 10 cm, 5 cm, and 4.2 m 2
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat sown at 270 plants/m2 in Henan field experiment.

claims.evidence_text: The sowing density, row spacing, sowing depth, and plot size were 270 plants m -2 , 10 cm, 5 cm, and 4.2 m 2
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat row spacing of 10 cm in Henan field experiment.

claims.evidence_text: The sowing density, row spacing, sowing depth, and plot size were 270 plants m -2 , 10 cm, 5 cm, and 4.2 m 2
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat sowing depth of 5 cm in Henan field experiment.

claims.evidence_text: The brown soil of the region had pH and bulk density of 7.11 and 1.38 g cm -3, respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat grown on brown soil of pH 7.11 in Henan.

claims.evidence_text: the mean phenological stages 235.21 d, 228.93 d, and 227.20 d, respectively
claims.claim_summary: Simulated winter wheat full phenological cycle ~227-235 days, shortening under warming.
